situation: Submitted a degree translation done by a bilingual colleague. Frankfurt Ausländerbehörde rejected the translation, stating only sworn/certified translators (vereidigte Übersetzer) are accepted for official immigration documents.
rejection_reason: Translation of degree certificate not performed by a court-sworn translator. Frankfurt requires all non-German documents to be translated by a certified sworn translator listed in the Justizministerium Hessen registry.
what_worked: Found a sworn translator via the justizministerium-hessen.de registry. Translation cost €120 and was completed in 3 days. Resubmitted documents and received Blue Card appointment within 2 weeks.
what_failed: Submitting a translation with a notary stamp (notarization is not equivalent to sworn translation). Using a professional translation agency without court-sworn status. Trying to submit the English-language degree directly.
